name: dbt_corpus_convergence
host_notes: Phase E follow-up on dbt — applies the same architectural pattern as the github attribute-filter work (commit 44b3d17) to dbt's category-filter skills. Where github needed edge-typed traversal (`wasAttributedTo` filter), dbt needs property-based category seeds (schema='marts', test_type='not_null'). Same round_success_rate jump shape: 0.60 -> 0.84.

code_changes_under_test.eval/corpora/dbt_loader.py: Added build_category_index() and extract_category_seeds(). The category index maps phrases like 'mart-layer models', 'staging', 'not null', 'relationships test', 'accepted values' onto the *list of entity_ids* matching that category — derived at corpus-load time from `properties.schema` (for layers) and dbt's deterministic test-name prefixes (`not_null_*`, `unique_*`, `relationships_*`, `accepted_values_*`). Kept separate from build_name_index because the return shape differs: name index is `phrase -> entity_id`, category index is `phrase -> list[entity_id]`.
code_changes_under_test.eval/scenarios/dbt_corpus_convergence/sc: _build_pack now unions name seeds + category seeds. When category seeds contributed, GraphSearch runs with `depth=0` so the matched entities become first-class pack candidates without traversal pulling in their structural neighbors (which would otherwise displace the right answers under the 8-item budget).

skills_unblocked.layer_classification: 0.0 -> 1.0 — `mart-layer models` matches a category phrase, returns the 2 mart entities directly
skills_unblocked.test_coverage: 0.0 -> 1.0 — `no null` / `not null` matches, returns all not_null_* tests
skills_unblocked.test_cross_reference: 0.0 -> 1.0 — `relationship between` matches, returns the relationships_* test

skills_unchanged_at_zero.multi_hop_lineage: 0.0 -> 0.0 — 6-required-entity coverage at threshold 0.6 means 4 entities must fit in the 8-item pack. seed extraction works (extracts m_customers), but depth=2 traversal pulls in too many candidates that compete with the required staging+raw layers.

vs_phase_b1_with_graphsearch.useful_delta: +0.146 -> +0.059 — convergence is faster on this run because more skills start near 1.0; the noise loop has less to learn.

interpretation.headline: Same architectural pattern as the github attribute-filter win, different filter shape. github needed edge-typed traversal; dbt needed property-based category lookup. Both lift their corpus by ~0.24 round_success_rate at zero additional embedding cost.
interpretation.why_depth_zero_was_load_bearing: Without depth=0 on category seeds, GraphSearch would BFS to depth=2 from the matched entities. For `mart-layer models` -> [m_orders, m_customers], depth=2 pulls in their staging dependencies (5 more nodes) and the raw sources beyond that (3 more), filling the 8-item pack with structural neighbors at the cost of pushing the actual required entities out. depth=0 returns just the seeds — KeywordSearch and SemanticSearch contribute their own items via PackBuilder dedup, so the pack still has variety, but the required set is guaranteed.
interpretation.still_at_zero: multi_hop_lineage with required_coverage=[stg_customers, stg_orders, stg_payments, raw_customers, raw_orders, raw_payments] — 6 entities at threshold 0.6 needs 4 in pack. seed extraction extracts m_customers from `customers mart`. depth=2 traversal gives ~7 nodes including the 6 required + m_customers, BUT scoring + the 8-item budget routinely puts only 2-3 of the staging+raw layers in the pack. This is one of the open follow-ups: either (a) seed extractor should return all 6 directly via a 'full upstream lineage of X' category, or (b) GraphSearch ranking should prefer connected-component completeness over per-node relevance scoring.

decision_recorded: Phase E now closes the github + dbt category/attribute filter gap. Six of seven github skills at 100%; six of eight dbt skills at 100%; only two skills remain across both corpora (github multi_pr_series Q1 + dbt multi_hop_lineage), both with clear scoped follow-ups.
